_provenance: v4 motion-packet rewrite 2026-05-16 per PROPOSAL_player_driven_argument.md §1 (Crab subsection) + §41 SPRINT_LOG checklist item 6. Crab reads envelope / renewal / renumbering / timeline as raw facts and does NOT name Article 135-bis until after Cula has surfaced the four required elements. The 135-bis citation moves from first_meeting_with_binder into a new post-packet state (crab_post_packet_names_article), gated on all four element_* bools. The three-tonal-options block on the v3 first-meeting state is dropped (per the new design, options are reserved for decoy decisions; the binder hand-off chains directly into the post-packet beat via top-level chain:true). Crab's single decoy moment per PROPOSAL §1.2 — the post-Halina incapacity offer — lands here as crab_post_halina_incapacity_offer + crab_post_halina_incapacity_refuses. Address forms unchanged: 'Dr. A. Cula' pre-recruit, bare 'Cula' post-recruit. before_binder gains an on_dismiss write that surfaces the resident-no-authority observation (Crab is on the stairwell, has seen the current occupant of #7); this auto-sets element_no_third_party_cure ahead of the binder pickup. The post-recruit chained 'crab_post_pitch_response' state from v3 is removed — its content is absorbed into crab_post_packet_names_article so 135-bis is named only after packet completeness.
_anti_ai_voice_pass: No contrastive antithesis. Em-dashes capped per Crab's gear-shift discipline. No scrub-list vocabulary. Postal-theatre, decorative-gaps, and standing-in-the-right-order calibration anchors preserved verbatim. The cold-refusal beat (crab_post_halina_incapacity_refuses) is built as a refusal, not a lecture, per Crab's voice agent §Calibration.

states._comment: Pre-binder first meeting. Crab is on the stairwell, has read the brass plate, and has seen the current occupant of #7. The 'postal theatre' anchor is the gear-shift closer. v4 addition: on_dismiss surfaces the resident-no-authority observation (E4 supporting evidence). This auto-sets element_no_third_party_cure via the per-state write pattern documented in PROPOSAL §3.2 (default: on_dismiss writes, no listener). The renumbering fact also surfaces here. Address form 'Dr. A. Cula' pre-recruit.
lines: Dr. A. Cula. Heard. Murrow's procedural binder?
Then we are early. I cannot tell you what is defective in the service until I have read the notice. The notice is in the binder. Until then I am standing in a doorway making faces.
Do. Renumbering was two thousand and fifteen. The envelope we have a copy of was sent to number seven. The building above us reads number twelve. That is not service. That is postal theatre.

And the door we are standing outside of — current resident is a young man, twenty-six or so, no relation to Mrs. Sikorska. He opened the envelope, saw it was not his, brought it down three weeks late. Nobody at this address has authority to receive process for her.

states._comment: Repeat-visit beat without binder. v4 strips the explicit 'Article one-thirty-five-bis in three colors' citation — Crab names the shelf and the flagging, not the doctrine. The Article moves to crab_post_packet_names_article.
lines: Dr. A. Cula. Still no binder. The shelf has been patient with you; I will be also.
Second shelf, blue spine. Murrow has flagged the relevant pages in three colors. Bring it down. Until then we are arguing from memory.

states._comment: v4 fused intro + binder hand-off. Cula greets first (AGENTS.md §First-meeting introductions), then names the binder. Crab reads three pages and the dates as raw facts — envelope (#7, 8 April), renewal (#12, 2019, landlord countersignature), renumbering (2015), notice receipt (28 April), and a pointer to Halina's records. Crab does NOT name Article 135-bis here. The Article is named only in crab_post_packet_names_article, which chains via top-level chain:true on this state. Options block dropped (the v3 tonal-options carried no payload differentiation; chain:true on the state itself fires the post-packet beat after dismissal). on_dismiss writes met_crab, recruited_crab, the three binder_read_* bools, the surfaced_notice_timeline pointer, the surfaced_payment_receipts pointer (Halina's records), the surfaced_resident_no_authority observation, and the three element bools Crab's read-aloud establishes (E1, E2, E3). Element E4 was already set by before_binder if the player took that path, but it is re-asserted here so the late-binder path (player went straight from Murrow to the archive without speaking to Crab first) also lands E4 correctly. `once: true` ensures the state doesn't re-fire on any unlikely re-entry after the chain has fired. Top-level chain:true triggers the post-dismiss re-fire that lands on crab_post_packet_names_article.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Crab. I'm Cula. The procedural binder — Murrow said you'd want a second pair of eyes on the Sikorska service before fourteen hundred.

lines: Dr. A. Cula. Heard. Hand it here.
Three pages worth reading. First — the envelope. Number seven. Dated the eighth of April.
Second — the renewal. Number twelve, two thousand and nineteen, landlord's countersignature on the page facing.
Third — the renumbering. Two thousand and fifteen. The building stopped being number seven eleven years ago.

And the timing. Notice dated the eighth; the resident at number seven walked it down on the twenty-eighth. Hearing already ran ex parte. Mrs. Sikorska has paperwork of her own, by the way — receipts, the kind you keep when you expect the landlord to forget.
Three problems, one notice, one Friday. I will take the post — register, return-to-sender slip, the current resident at number seven. You take the drafting.

states._comment: v4 late-binder path. Cula already met Crab pre-binder (before_binder fired and set met_crab + element_no_third_party_cure); this state is the work hand-off. Same facts, no Article, chain:true on the state itself fires the post-packet beat. on_dismiss covers the binder reads and the remaining three element bools.
lines: Heard. Hand it here.
Envelope: number seven, eighth of April. Renewal: number twelve, two thousand and nineteen, countersigned. Renumbering: two thousand and fifteen.
Notice receipt the twenty-eighth. Mrs. Sikorska keeps receipts.
Three problems, one notice, one Friday. I will take the post. You take the drafting.

states.trigger: chapter1.recruited_crab == true && chapter1.element_non_current_address == true && chapter1.element_landlord_knowledge == true && chapter1.element_timely_actual_notice_motion == true && chapter1.element_no_third_party_cure == true && !chapter1.halina_met && !chapter1.entered_court
states._comment: v4 NEW state — post-packet naming of Article 135-bis. Fires after first_meeting_with_binder or after_binder_first_engagement dismisses (state-level chain:true on those triggers the post-dismiss re-fire that lands here). Crab now names the Article — translated, with the gear-shift closer ('a confession with a postal date') and the canonical 'three problems' closer. This is the only place in the live crab.json catalogue where Article 135-bis is cited. Address form: bare 'Cula' (post-recruit, private firm context). once:true.
lines: Cula. The four parts are in. Article one-thirty-five-bis, paragraph two of the Code of Civil Procedure — translated, he sent a writ to a building that has not been Mrs. Sikorska's building in eleven years, to a door whose occupant could not accept it for her, eleven days late on her actual notice. A confession with a postal date.
Drafting takes an afternoon. The walking takes Friday. Find me before fourteen hundred if anything in the binder reads wrong.

states._comment: v4 NEW state — D5 (incapacity) decision moment per PROPOSAL §1.2. Crab post-Halina is the single canonical surface for the incapacity offer; the proposal explicitly notes 'a single Cula-internal option in the Crab post-Halina-meeting state.' Cula floats the idea in his own voice (the player is the one offering the blunder, not Crab); options write chapter1.decoy_incapacity. Both branches chain via options.chain:true — the 'yes' value lands the cold refusal in crab_post_halina_incapacity_refuses; the 'no' value falls through to hint_needs_archive on the chain re-fire. once:true so the offer cannot be re-asked.
lines: Cula. The post is walked. The drafting is yours. Anything else before I go back out?

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: [Mrs. Sikorska is seventy-one. Belt-and-braces — could we plead incapacity as a fallback?]

choices.text: Drop it. The procedural angle does the work; her age is not the argument.
Add incapacity as a fallback. Insurance.

states._comment: v4 NEW state — cold refusal when decoy_incapacity is set. Crab withdraws from the drafting in voice; the actual recruited_crab flag-reset is a battle-controller concern per PROPOSAL §1.2 in_fiction_consequence and stays out of this Design pass. The dialogue itself is the pre-court punishment beat: Crab calls Cula out, names the insult, and walks. Address form returns to 'Dr. A. Cula' for this state — the friend-form is suspended. once:true. The line 'older than the building' is the calibration anchor for Crab's refusal voice; the closer ('a courtesy to Mr. Murrow') is the gear-shift that keeps the post commitment in fiction without rehabilitating the choice.
lines: Dr. A. Cula. No.
No. Mrs. Sikorska is older than the building and sharper than most of the lawyers in the courtroom on Friday. She is not incompetent. The argument insults her, and I will not draft to it.
Find me again when the argument is something else. I will still walk the post. The post is a courtesy to Mr. Murrow, not to you.

states._comment: V1.4 coffee reaction — S-grade, post-recruit. Line preserved verbatim from minigames.txt §Character reactions §Crab Perfect ('No obvious flaws. Disturbing.'); the gear-shift is built in — procedural verdict + colloquial closer. Crab addresses Cula by bare 'Cula' per AGENTS.md §Address forms (post-recruit, private context).
lines: Cula. No obvious flaws. Disturbing.

states._comment: V1.4 coffee reaction — D/F-grade, post-recruit. Line preserved verbatim from minigames.txt §Character reactions §Crab Bad ('That is not coffee. That is witness intimidation.'). Crab uses bare 'Cula' (post-recruit).
lines: Cula. That is not coffee. That is witness intimidation.

states._comment: V1.4 coffee reaction — S-grade, pre-recruit. Same line as the post-recruit variant; address form switches to 'Dr. A. Cula' per AGENTS.md. Practically unreachable in normal play.
lines: Dr. A. Cula. No obvious flaws. Disturbing.
Dr. A. Cula. That is not coffee. That is witness intimidation.

states._comment: Post-Halina-meeting hint state. Crab's factual domain is the service certificate; he nudges Cula toward the archive. Bare 'Cula' (post-recruit). Unchanged from v3.
lines: Cula. The service address is in the archive — somewhere. Someone filed a certificate at the cooperative office when the building was resurveyed. We need to know whether the certificate describes the door on the envelope.
The paper is the next step. Until we have it the case is half-told.

states._comment: Quiet approval beat between archive research and the readiness check. 'Not beautiful but standing in the right order' is the anti-reassurance calibration anchor — it is a fact, not comfort. Bare 'Cula' per AGENTS.md.
lines: Cula. The facts are not beautiful. They are standing in the right order. That is what we needed before fourteen hundred.

states._comment: Catch-all repeat-beat for post-recruitment states not matched above. 'Decorative gaps' is the gear-shift closer; Crab names absence-of-evidence as a procedural problem. Bare 'Cula' per AGENTS.md.
lines: Cula. If we go in now we will be making an argument with decorative gaps. The argument can be made. The gaps are what the landlord will fill, and he will fill them in his favour.

idle_flavor: The office is quiet. Either nothing has happened or the evidence is hiding better than usual.
Half the legal questions in this office begin with someone reading the address on an envelope twice.
